title: Electric current
short: The rate at which charge flows past a point, I = Q / t, measured in amperes (A).
full:
Electric current is the flow of electric charge: the amount of charge passing a point per unit time, I = Q/t. One ampere is one coulomb per second. By convention current points in the direction positive charge would move, opposite to the actual drift of electrons in a wire.

In a simple circuit the current is set by the driving voltage and the resistance, I = U/R (Ohm's law).

The SI unit is the ampere (A).
